title: Call of Duty
edition_pinned: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare (Infinity Ward, November 2007) — design-canonical multiplayer: create-a-class, XP unlocks, Prestige, killstreaks (3/5/7). Later scorestreaks and specialists are descendants, not the pin.
moat: Killstreaks convert a no-death kill chain into map-scale power while XP unlocks reshape the next loadout.

loop_skeleton.description: Spawn with a create-a-class loadout, fight short lives in a mode map, chain kills without dying to earn UAV/airstrike/helicopter streaks, die and reset the streak, bank match XP to unlock weapons, attachments, perks, and Prestige resets.

depth_mechanisms.name: killstreak ladder
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Three kills without dying call a UAV, five an airstrike, seven a helicopter. Death zeroes streak progress. Designers replaced zone-based air support with streaks so deathmatch flow was not broken by map trigger camping.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If killstreak rewards are removed, players lose a mid-match incentive to extend lives and risk for map-scale support rather than resetting after every engagement.

depth_mechanisms.name: create-a-class perk loadout
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Custom classes pick primary, sidearm, attachments, special grenade, and three perk tiers. Loadout is chosen before the match so weapon hunting mid-map is not required.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If players cannot preset classes and must loot weapons only on the map, time-to-chosen-weapon firefight rises and perk-defined loadouts disappear.

depth_mechanisms.name: xp prestige treadmill
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Match actions grant XP that ranks the player and unlocks weapons, attachments, and camos. Hitting the cap enables Prestige: reset rank for an insignia and a new climb (up to ten on consoles).
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If XP unlocks and Prestige are removed and all weapons are free at rank 1, second-session goals stop being loadout-ladder climbs and reduce to match score alone.

feel_signature: A life is a short commit from spawn; each kill without dying ticks a streak meter toward a map-scale reward; death zeroes the meter and returns the player to spawn with the same class.
second_session_answer: Players return to finish the next weapon unlock or Prestige rank and to practice maps and classes — goals that persist outside any single match outcome.
packaging_lessons: Ship deathmatch-readable maps and preset classes before mode bloat; COD4 maps were built around deathmatch layouts first.
Put mid-match power on no-death chains (killstreaks), not on contested air-support trigger zones that stall deathmatch flow.
anti_lessons: Do not copy killstreaks into a PvE campaign without redesign — the mechanism assumes respawn-based multiplayer opponents.
Do not layer pay-to-unlock weapons onto the XP ladder if the design goal is skill-and-time progression (later remaster microtransactions are a negative lesson).

system_bom.note: spawn-fight-die cycle is the match atom that feeds streak progress or XP bank
maps and modes route spawns, objectives, and encounter ranges so short lives keep recycling
match XP drives unlock rank between lives and matches
weapons, attachments, perks, and camos unlock by rank and reshape the next create-a-class
Prestige resets rank for long-horizon goals after the unlock ladder is complete
death resets streak but not XP; match score is separate from personal unlock goals
combat HUD exposes objectives, ammo, stance, grenades, and damage direction during short lives; create-a-class is a pre-match loadout surface (not claimed from the combat manual alone)
personal performance and kill/death ratio emphasis sustain return beyond a single match win; platform leaderboard functionality is not claimed

citations.claim: Edition pin: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare released November 2007; multiplayer introduced XP leveling, Prestige, create-a-class, perks, and killstreaks that became series hallmarks.
Killstreak ladder: three-kill UAV, five-kill airstrike, seven-kill helicopter; death resets streak progress.
Killstreak origin: team replaced zone air-support triggers with killstreaks because zone design hurt deathmatch flow (Game Informer multiplayer design coverage via Wikipedia).
Create-a-class perk loadout: primary, sidearm, attachments, special grenade, three perks; preset before match.
XP prestige treadmill: experience unlocks content; Prestige resets rank for insignia up to ten times on consoles.
Maps designed primarily around deathmatch layouts that also support other modes.
Development postmortem context: Infinity Ward multiplayer design priorities and two-year development notes.
Feel signature and loop: short multiplayer lives with killstreaks, create-a-class, and XP unlocks as hallmarks of the multiplayer template.
GamesRadar review: multiplayer leveling unlocks weapons and content; killstreaks and class system support short-life multiplayer loop.
Xbox 360 manual: HUD shows objectives, ammo, stance, grenades, and damage direction during combat (create-a-class screens and streak callouts are not claimed from this manual).
Play-loop BOM: free-for-all and team modes recycle short lives with objectives ranging from deathmatch to control points.
Multiplayer BOM: maps and modes are the required surface for spawn routing and encounter recycling.
XP and progression BOM: experience unlocks weapons, attachments, perks, and camos by rank.
Meta-progression BOM: Prestige reset creates long-horizon goals after the rank cap.
Win-loss-death BOM: death resets streak progress while XP continues to bank toward unlocks; match outcome is separate from personal unlock goals.
UI BOM (narrowed): combat HUD exposes combat state during short lives per the manual; create-a-class is documented on multiplayer overview sources, not the combat HUD manual.
Personal-performance BOM: retrospective coverage notes renewed emphasis on personal performance and kill-to-death ratio alongside unlock progression; leaderboard functionality is not claimed.
Packaging lesson 1: deathmatch-first map design before mode bloat.
Packaging lesson 2: mid-match power on no-death killstreaks rather than contested air-support trigger zones that hurt deathmatch flow.
Anti-lesson killstreaks in PvE: mechanism assumes multiplayer respawn opponents.
Anti-lesson pay-to-unlock: remaster microtransactions are a negative descendant of the skill-and-time XP ladder.
